questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
options: Earth moves closer to and further from the Sun.
The Moon passes in front of the Sun every 24 hours.
Cloud cover changes the amount of sunlight in space.
questions.explanation: Day and night happen because Earth spins, so different parts face the Sun at different times.
questions.senNote: Day and night happen because Earth spins, so different parts face the Sun at different times.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why satellites stay in orbit
questions.question: Why does a satellite stay in orbit around Earth?
options: There is no gravity in space, so the satellite floats in a straight line.
Air resistance above Earth pushes the satellite around the orbit.
The satellite engine must keep firing all the time to hold the orbit.
Gravity provides the centripetal force needed to keep the satellite moving in a curved path.
questions.correctAnswer: Gravity provides the centripetal force needed to keep the satellite moving in a curved path.
questions.explanation: Gravity constantly pulls the satellite towards Earth, changing its direction and keeping it in orbit.
questions.senNote: Gravity constantly pulls the satellite towards Earth, changing its direction and keeping it in orbit.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Identify a use of geostationary satellites
questions.question: Which is a common use of geostationary satellites?
options: Studying close-up images of distant galaxies
Exploring the surface of Mars directly
Measuring the magnetic field inside Earth's core
questions.explanation: Geostationary satellites stay above the same point on Earth, making them useful for communications and broadcasting.
questions.senNote: Geostationary satellites stay above the same point on Earth, making them useful for communications and broadcasting.
